entries.title: The Southern Poverty Law Center built a fortune opposing Trump during his first term. The president is now trying to crush it. The antiracism group faces federal charges it says are bogus.
Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin faces a difficult task: improve the department’s image while still implementing President Trump’s central campaign promise of mass deportations
A Connecticut Republican quit the governor’s race after a report alleged she used a government credit card for tens of thousands of dollars in personal purchases while mayor of New Britain
A bill that would create a pathway for crypto to further upend the world of traditional finance moved one step closer to law
Pentagon leaders say they could start running out of money for operations this summer unless Congress passes a new wartime spending bill
Republican and Democratic senators unanimously adopted a resolution to suspend their pay during future government shutdowns
More than 100 U.S. ambassador posts remain vacant under Trump, raising concerns about America’s diplomatic reach abroad.
Todd Blanche, the acting head of the Justice Department, isn’t wasting any time in his audition to be the next U.S. attorney general
The WSJ used both AI analysis and traditional reporting to decipher President Trump’s Truth Social behavior. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics newsletter:
The Republican conundrum: Even as Trump’s congressional allies wrestle with keeping him focused on the economy, they can’t agree on a strategy to address voters’ concerns
Trump administration health officials are walking a tightrope on hantavirus: trying to avoid public panic and a repeat of what they view as pandemic-era missteps
Denise Powell Wins Tight Democratic Primary for Open House Seat in Nebraska
A Nebraska state senator and a former communications executive were in a tight Democratic congressional primary early Wednesday for a House seat left open by retiring GOP Rep. Don Bacon
Louisiana Republican Rep. Julia Letlow is locked in a bitter primary that is testing the president’s power to shape MAGA’s next generation
An effort from Democrats to redraw maps in more states comes as two court rulings shatter the party’s optimism for beating Republicans in a gerrymandering battle
Republicans are playing defense in Ohio and a growing number of other red states, where Democrats are counting on voter backlash to power them to victory this fall in their fight to retake the Senate
The Pentagon abruptly canceled deployment of an armored brigade to Europe, a major step toward shrinking the U.S. military posture in Europe that caught some military officials by surprise
FBI Director Kash Patel fielded questions from lawmakers about allegations that he drinks excessively and other issues during a Senate hearing
The new battleship to be named after Trump will be nuclear-powered, a move expected to boost the cost and complexity of a project that was facing questions about its feasibility
President Trump privately complained to the acting attorney general about media leaks in the wake of the Iran war last month, prompting an aggressive push at the DOJ to pursue those investigations
President Trump escalated his public feud with Pope Leo XIV, accusing the pontiff of endangering Catholics by opposing U.S. military action against Iran
